title: Real-time Reverse Transcription Polymerase Chain Reaction (RT-qPCR)
author: Izabela M Rezende
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.pw8dphw

steps.title: Each sample is tested at least in duplicate, or triplicate
steps.instruction: Each sample is tested at least in duplicate, or triplicate.

steps.title: The final reaction volume in this protocol is 20µl
steps.instruction: The final reaction volume in this protocol is 20µl.

steps.title: Estimate the appropriate amount of each reagent for each test/sample
steps.instruction: Estimate the appropriate amount of each reagent for each test/sample.

steps.title: Determine the number of reactions to...
steps.instruction: Determine the number of reactions to be set up, including negative control from the RNA extraction, for the non-template control and for the positive control reactions.

steps.title: Add 1 or 2 reactions to...
steps.instruction: Add 1 or 2 reactions to this number to compensate for pipetting error.

steps.title: Prepare the reaction mix (minus the...
steps.instruction: Prepare the reaction mix (minus the RNA template) by combining the GoTaq® Probe qPCR Master Mix with dUTP, GoScript™ RT Mix for 1-Step RT-qPCR, primers, hydrolysis probe and Nuclease-Free Water as described below. Vortex briefly to mix. %s %s *The GoTaq® Probe qPCR Master Mix included in the GoTaq® Probe 1-Step RT-qPCR System is formulated with dUTP. When dUTP is incorporated into the amplification products, the amplicons are susceptible to degradation by uracilDNA glycosylase (UNG); this allows you to incorporate UNG into subsequent reactions to control possible carryover contamination.

steps.title: Add the appropriate volume of reaction...
steps.instruction: Add the appropriate volume of reaction mix (without the RNA template) to each PCR tube or well of an optical grade PCR plate.

steps.title: Add the RNA template (or water...
steps.instruction: Add the RNA template (or water for the no-template control reactions) to the appropriate wells of the reaction plate.

steps.title: Seal the tubes or optical plate;...
steps.instruction: Seal the tubes or optical plate; centrifuge briefly to collect the contents of the wells at the bottom. Protect from extended light exposure or elevated temperatures before cycling. The samples are ready for thermal cycling.

steps.title: When the cycling ends, analyze the...
steps.instruction: When the cycling ends, analyze the results according to the manufacturer's instructions.

steps.title: RNA extraction control and non-template controls...
steps.instruction: RNA extraction control and non-template controls must be negative and positive control must be positive.

steps.title: To be considered positive a sample...
steps.instruction: To be considered positive a sample must present at least two replicates with amplification equal of bellow the cycle threshold of 37.
